mission.title: consul: Connect Vault CA token updates are not retained on restart
mission.description: Connect Vault CA token updates are not retained on restart. This issue affects 9+ users.

steps.title: Identify consul troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your consul deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul -n consul --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `"Failed to initialize secondary CA provider: error configuring provider: Error making API request.`

steps.title: Review consul configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant consul configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul
```
When filing a bug, please include the following headings if possible. Any example text in this template can be deleted.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Connect Vault CA token updates are not retained on restart
steps.description:
This fixes a bug that was identified, which resulted in subsequent ConnectCA configuration updates in secondary DC's not persisting in the cluster.
```yaml
agent:
	check_monitors = 0
	check_ttls = 0
	checks = 3
	services = 3
build:
	prerelease = 
	revision = c976ffd2
	version = 1.10.3
consul:
	acl = enabled
	bootstrap = false
	known_datacenters = 3
	leader = true
	leader_addr = 172.16.18.231:8300
	server = true
raft:
	applied_index = 3459907
	commit_index = 3459907
	fsm_pending = 0
	last_contact = 0
	last_log_index = 3459907
	last_log_term = 31964
	last_snapshot_index = 3459154
	last_snapshot_term = 31964
	latest_configuration = [{Suffrage:Voter ID:0db759ad-0007-2ea4-4d41-62aff64515d5 Address:172.16.18.231:8300} {Suffrage:Voter ID:4de69dad-f717-c
```

steps.title: Confirm Connect Vault CA token updates are not retained… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul -n consul --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `"Failed to initialize secondary CA provider: error configuring provider: Error making API request.` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: This fixes a bug that was identified, which resulted in subsequent ConnectCA configuration updates in secondary DC's not persisting in the cluster.

Code: 403. Errors:

* permission denied"

> consul connect ca get-config > connect_ca.json
# Edit connect_ca.json with new token
> consul connect ca set-config -config-file connect_ca.json
Error setting CA configuration: Unexpected response code: 500 (backend not initialized)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with consul installed or the issue environment reproducible.
